name: From Vistabella del Maestrat to Xodos through the Penyagolosa Natural Park – Circular gravel
name_original: De Vistabella del Maestrat a Xodos por el Parque Natural de Penyagolosa – Circular gravel
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Vistabella Del Maestrat traverse the highest municipality in the Valencian Community, situated at an altitude of 1,246 meters within the Maestrazgo massif. The region is characterized by imposing mountains, extensive woodlands, and diverse terrain, including both paved and unpaved segments. It lies within the Penyagolosa Natural Park, offering a rich natural environment with varied landscapes. This area provides challenging routes with significant elevation gains. Best touring cycling routes around Vistabella Del Maestrat The most popular touring cycling route is From Vistabella del Maestrat to Xodos through the Penyagolosa Natural Park – Circular gravel, a 24.1 miles (38.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 51 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural park's varied gravel paths. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Culla Historic Village – Font d'en Segures loop from Benassal, a difficult 19.0 miles (30.6 km) path. This route features scenic views of the historic Culla village and surrounding landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Roman Bridge loop from Mas de Cotanda, a 21.8 miles (35.1 km) trail leading through the River Montlleó area, often completed in about 3 hours 4 minutes. Touring cycling around Vistabella Del Maestrat is defined by high-altitude mountain terrain, the natural beauty of Penyagolosa Natural Park, and historic village charm. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels, with more than 30 routes available. The routes in Vistabella Del Maestrat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Vistabella Del Maestrat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level for touring cycling routes around Vistabella Del Maestrat?
A: Touring cycling around Vistabella Del Maestrat is primarily suited for experienced cyclists. The region features 32 difficult routes and 3 moderate routes, with no easy options available. This is due to the high-altitude mountain terrain and significant elevation gains within the Maestrazgo massif and Penyagolosa Natural Park.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes offer a circular experience. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938365649/from-vistabella-del-maestrat-to-xodos-through-the-penyagolosa-natural-park-circular-gravel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Vistabella del Maestrat to Xodos through the Penyagolosa Natural Park – Circular gravel&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 24.1-mile (38.8 km) loop exploring the natural park's varied gravel paths. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31847197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge loop from Mas de Cotanda&lt;/a&gt;, a 21.8-mile (35.1 km) difficult trail.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Vistabella Del Maestrat?
A: The terrain around Vistabella Del Maestrat is diverse, ranging from paved surfaces to unpaved and gravel segments. As the highest municipality in the Valencian Community, you can expect imposing mountains and significant elevation changes. Routes often traverse extensive woodlands and the rich natural environment of the Penyagolosa Natural Park.

Q: What historical and natural landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in both natural beauty and historical sites. You can encounter the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1113753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Culla Historic Village&lt;/a&gt;, known for its medieval charm. Many routes also pass by the ancient Roman Bridge over the River Montlleó. Within the Penyagolosa Natural Park, you'll find extraordinary beauty, diverse wildlife, and extensive woodlands. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5617937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vistabella del Maestrat&lt;/a&gt; itself retains much of its medieval character with old town walls and gates.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain passes for touring cyclists in the area?
A: Yes, Vistabella del Maestrat, being in a mountainous region, offers several challenging passes. Notable passes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3595893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertomingalvo Pass&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2809664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port del Collao (Coll del Vidre)&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3890802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xodos / Chodos Pass&lt;/a&gt;. These passes contribute to the significant elevation gains found on many routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Vistabella Del Maestrat?
A: Given its high altitude and mountainous terrain, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for touring cycling in Vistabella Del Maestrat. These periods offer milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential snow or colder conditions in winter. Always check local weather forecasts before your ride.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Vistabella Del Maestrat?
A: The touring cycling routes in Vistabella Del Maestrat are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging high-altitude mountain terrain, the natural beauty of Penyagolosa Natural Park, and the charm of the historic villages encountered along the way.

Q: Are there any routes that explore other historic villages besides Vistabella del Maestrat?
A: Absolutely. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20139284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Culla Historic Village – Font d'en Segures loop from Benassal&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the scenic historic village of Culla. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesuela del Cid – Tower of La Iglesuela del Cid loop from Vilafranca&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the charming village of Iglesuela del Cid.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available in the area?
A: One of the longer and more challenging routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesuela del Cid – Tower of La Iglesuela del Cid loop from Vilafranca&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 43.6 miles (70.2 km) and features significant elevation gain.

Q: Is public transport available for cyclists in Vistabella Del Maestrat?
A: While Vistabella del Maestrat is a somewhat remote, high-altitude municipality, some bus services connect it to larger towns like Castellón de la Plana. It's advisable to check with specific bus operators, such as Hicid SA, regarding their bicycle transport policy and availability, as this can vary. Planning your route to start and end in Vistabella del Maestrat or a nearby village is often the most straightforward approach.

Q: Are there specific regulations for cycling within Penyagolosa Natural Park?
A: Penyagolosa Natural Park is a protected area, and while cycling is generally permitted on designated trails and roads, it's always recommended to respect park regulations. This includes staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, and carrying out all waste. Specific permits are not typically required for general cycling, but always check for any updated local guidelines, especially for larger groups or organized events.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: As Vistabella del Maestrat is the highest municipality in the Valencian Community, touring cycling routes offer expansive panoramic views of the Maestrazgo massif and the surrounding natural park. You'll encounter imposing mountains, extensive woodlands, and picturesque landscapes, including glimpses of traditional farmhouses and medieval villages. The El Calvario Chapel, for instance, provides impressive panoramic views of the municipality itself.
